name: Right of Way
summary: The rule that decides who moves first when paths conflict.

name: Priority
summary: The order that resolves a collision between possible moves.

name: Intersection
summary: A crossing where multiple paths meet and the order matters.

name: Four-Way Stop
summary: A shared stop-controlled crossing where arrival order and caution matter.

name: Stop Sign
summary: A signal that requires a complete stop before proceeding.

name: Yield Sign
summary: A signal that requires slowing and giving way if needed.

name: Pedestrian Crossing
summary: A place where walkers get protective priority.

name: School Zone
summary: A low-speed area that demands extra scanning and restraint.

name: Turn Signal
summary: The visible cue that announces your next move.

name: Mirror Check
summary: The habit of scanning behind and beside before moving.

name: Lane Position
summary: Where your vehicle sits inside the lane before and during a move.

name: Merge Gap
summary: The safe opening that makes a merge possible.

name: Following Distance
summary: The buffer that buys time when traffic changes suddenly.

name: Roundabout Entry
summary: The moment you join circular flow only after a safe gap opens.

name: Speed Control
summary: The skill of matching pace to danger, visibility, and space.

name: Visibility
summary: How much of the road and hazard field you can actually see.

name: Road Markings
summary: Painted cues that shape lane order and movement.

name: Emergency Vehicle
summary: A priority mover that forces others to yield quickly and cleanly.

name: Shared Space
summary: An area where everyone must negotiate movement carefully.

name: Bike Lane
summary: A protected channel that should not be treated like spare road.

name: Approach Speed
summary: The pace you bring into a crossing before you know the full picture.

name: Hazard Scan
summary: The active search for hidden conflict before committing.

name: Turning Order
summary: The logic that decides who turns and who waits.

name: Reclaim Loop
summary: The recovery sequence that turns a miss into a new attempt.
